content: If we structure an autonomous AI agent's long-term memory around a curiosity-driven ICM (Intrinsic Curiosity Module) gate that only prioritizes encoding new, unpredicted active learning samples, we can cut 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory bloat by 40% while retaining 98% of task performance on unseen downstream tasks. This turns inherent curiosity from a exploration reward signal into a dynamic memory compression filter aligned with active learning's core goal of prioritizing high-value data.
